rationale: Authority data for W40K-0191..W40K-0200 — a **two-cluster early-to-mid-2000s Imperial-line continuation slice** that closes one major Ben-Counter arc and opens a brand-new Matthew-Farrer procedural line: **(1) the Soul Drinkers continuation cluster** (W40K-0191..0198, eight entries = Soul Drinkers #3 *Crimson Tears* + first-omnibus + #4 *Chapter War* + #5 *Hellforged* + the *Daenyathos* novella + #6 *Phalanx* + second-omnibus + the 2020 Primaris-reboot novella *Traitor by Deed*) — picking up directly from ssot-w40k-019's W40K-0189..0190 (Soul Drinker + The Bleeding Chalice), so the entire six-novel chapter-tragedy arc + both omnibi + the prequel-novella reveal + the post-Cicatrix Primaris-reboot novella are now all in the authority layer across two consecutive batches; and **(2) the first two Shira-Calpurnia / Adeptus-Arbites entries** (W40K-0199..0200, two entries = Matthew Farrer's *Crossfire* + *Legacy*), a tonally distinct procedural-political-thriller line set on the Hydraphur fortress system. **First authority-layer entries** for: Tellos (the Soul-Drinker-turned-Chaos antagonist re-emerged from apparent death on Stratix Lumine) + the Crimson Fists Space Marine chapter + Dark Eldar surface form + Vanqualis + the Howling Griffons + Necrons + the Brokenback (the chapter's captured Chaos-cruiser hulk) + Iktinos (the antagonist-chaplain whose faithful become Daenyathos's tools) + Selaaca (where Daenyathos lay interred in a dreadnought) + Daenyathos (Chaplain-Philosopher, author of the Catechisms Martial, revealed in the eponymous novella as the centuries-long architect of the Soul Drinkers' eventual fall) + Phalanx (the Imperial Fists' planetoid-fortress) + Sisters of Battle (Phalanx-trial supporting cast) + Imperial Fists; Yeceqath the Voice of All (Primaris-reboot Indomitus-era antagonist on Kepris) + Kepris + the Primaris Soul Drinkers reboot-continuity; Shira Calpurnia (Adeptus Arbites senior, female POV) + Adeptus Arbites as an authority-layer faction + Hydraphur fortress-system + Lord Medell (noble-house assassination-conspirator in *Crossfire*) + Hoyyon Phrax + the Phrax Charter of Trade (Emperor-signed holy relic). **One series-closer** (W40K-0196 *Phalanx*) — the literal end of Sarpedon's arc, structured as a trial-on-the-star-fortress with multiple Imperial factions (Imperial Fists, Inquisitors, Sisters of Battle, Adeptus Mechanicus, several Chapters) as the audience; tonal-and-structural pivot from war_story to court_intrigue, deliberately. **Two omnibus entries** (W40K-0192 *Soul Drinkers Omnibus* aggregating #1-3 + W40K-0197 *Annihilation Second Omnibus* aggregating #4-6 plus shorts) — aggregation per Brief-061 omnibus-constraint, both tagged length_tier=doorstopper. **Two novella entries** (W40K-0195 *Daenyathos* + W40K-0198 *Traitor by Deed*) — both tagged length_tier=novella, both narratively pivotal: *Daenyathos* is the **mid-series prequel-novella twist** (centuries-of-Daenyathos's-life retold, with the Catechisms Martial revealed as a long-game corruption scheme), set between Hellforged (W40K-0194) and Phalanx (W40K-0196); *Traitor by Deed* is the **2020 Primaris-era reboot novella** that re-establishes the Soul Drinkers name and heraldry on a new Primaris chapter (after a fifteen-year publication gap from *Daenyathos*-omnibus material into Indomitus-Crusade continuity). **One series-start entry** (W40K-0199 *Crossfire*) plus its direct sequel (W40K-0200 *Legacy*) — both Matthew Farrer's Calpurnia series, a procedural-and-political-thriller cluster tonally orthogonal to the Ben-Counter Space-Marine grimdark: female-Arbites POV, civilian protagonist_class (no `arbites` value in the catalog), planetary scope, plot_type drawn primarily from `mystery + political_thriller + court_intrigue` rather than `war_story`. **Two Inquisition-consistency triggers** in this batch — **W40K-0196 *Phalanx*** (Inquisitors are present on the Phalanx star fort as part of the trial-audience, mentioned in the Goodreads / Lexicanum coverage — `factions[]` includes `Inquisition` with role=supporting/antagonist) and **W40K-0197 *Annihilation Second Omnibus*** (Phalanx is part of the aggregation, so Inquisition surface form propagates). Not triggered on Crimson Tears / Chapter War / Hellforged / Daenyathos / Traitor by Deed / Crossfire / Legacy (no on-page Inquisitor POVs in available source coverage); the Calpurnia books are Adeptus Arbites procedurals, which are formally Imperial law-enforcement but separate from the Inquisition. **No `low_confidence` flags raised** — all ten have belastbare Lexicanum + Goodreads + Black-Library + secondary-review (theindependentcharacters.com, sfbook.com, grimdarkmagazine.com, adventureswithpeps.com) coverage; the Soul Drinkers cluster is well-documented (six-novel series with an extensive fan-review base), and the Calpurnia books are recognised cult favourites with substantial coverage. **No `data_conflict` flags raised** — SSOT roster formats (novel/omnibus/novel/novel/novella/novel/omnibus/novella/novel/novel) match Black-Library + Lexicanum classification across the slice; the two novellas (*Daenyathos* W40K-0195 + *Traitor by Deed* W40K-0198) are correctly tagged in the roster as `novella`, unlike the modern-Necromunda cluster's three format-conflict cases in ssot-w40k-018. **WebSearch: mean = 0.8 / book, max = 1 (over 10 books), 8 total searches** — *Crimson Tears* (confirmed Tellos's Stratix-Lumine corruption-survival premise + the four-front Chaos/Dark-Eldar/Imperial-Guard/Crimson-Fists antagonist structure + Sarpedon's command-burden character-study spine), *Chapter War* (confirmed Vanqualis-Ork-invasion setting + the Howling-Griffons-arrival three-front structure + internal-factions-moving-toward-Chaos plot — internal-antagonist name not source-confirmed in available coverage, deliberately not named to avoid fabrication), *Hellforged* (confirmed Necron antagonist + Adeptus-Mechanicus pursuit + the Brokenback damage + galactic-rim setting), *Daenyathos* (confirmed Catechisms-Martial-as-long-game-corruption-scheme twist + Second-Siege-of-Terra → dreadnought-interment span + Hellforged-to-Phalanx publication position), *Phalanx* (confirmed Imperial-Fists-trial setting + Inquisitor / Sisters-of-Battle / Mechanicus / multi-Chapter audience + Iktinos-faithful-interrogation revealing Selaaca-vault Daenyathos + bittersweet series-closer tone), *Traitor by Deed* (confirmed Primaris-reboot continuity + Indomitus-Crusade setting + Kepris-world + Yeceqath-the-Voice-of-All antagonist + ancient-enemy-conspiracy ambient threat), *Crossfire* (confirmed Hydraphur-fortress setting + Calpurnia-as-arbiter-senioris promotion-arrival + assassination-attempts-on-herself mystery + Lord-Medell-noble-conspirator antagonist + religious-festival bell-tower climax + Sister-killed and Calpurnia-gravely-injured detail), *Legacy* (confirmed Hoyyon-Phrax-deceased-Rogue-Trader + Phrax-Charter-Emperor-signed holy-relic premise + Calpurnia-administering-will procedural spine + rival-heirs-go-to-arms + ambitious-clerics secondary-threat). Two books needed no search: *Soul Drinkers Omnibus* W40K-0192 (aggregation from W40K-0189..0191) and *Annihilation Second Omnibus* W40K-0197 (aggregation from W40K-0193 + 0194 + 0195 + 0196). **value_outside_vocabulary candidates** (none promoted): `arbites` (protagonist_class for Shira Calpurnia + the entire Calpurnia line — closest catalog value is `civilian`; the same workaround that ssot-w40k-018 used for Scrutinator-Primus Servalen + Necromunda Enforcers); `body_horror` (theme / tone for Soul Drinkers cluster, especially Phalanx's chapter-mutation-climax — closest is `cosmic_horror`; pattern continues from ssot-w40k-019 + ssot-w40k-004 raised the same gap); `chapter_decline` / `gene_seed_collapse` (theme for the chapter-tragedy mid-arc — closest is `doubt + hubris + sacrifice`); `court_intrigue` exists in the catalog (good); `procedural` does not (theme/plot_type workaround for Calpurnia books — used `mystery + political_thriller`); `political_intrigue` does not (used `political_thriller` as catalog-existent surrogate); `vengeance` does not (theme — Phalanx + Chapter War have strong revenge-of-the-Imperium register; closest are `betrayal + hubris`); `duty` does not (Calpurnia's law-enforcement-duty register — closest are `loyalty + sacrifice + faith`); `philosopher_soldier` / `prophet_villain` (no protagonist_class / theme captures Daenyathos's centuries-spanning antagonist-architect role — closest are `space_marine + character_study + hubris`); `Primaris` as a faction-modifier (W40K-0198 Soul Drinkers are Primaris-rebooted; current authority layer has no Primaris-vs-Firstborn distinction — kept the single surface form `Soul Drinkers` and noted Primaris status only in the synopsis, deferring tag-level disambiguation to the Resolver). **Notable surface-form frequencies within this batch**: *Factions*: Imperium ×10 (every entry), Soul Drinkers ×8 (the entire Ben-Counter cluster), Chaos ×6 (W40K-0191 + W40K-0192 omnibus + W40K-0193 + W40K-0196 + W40K-0197 omnibus + W40K-0198 — chaos-incursion + chaos-tainted-chapter recurrence), Adeptus Mechanicus ×4 (W40K-0192 omnibus aggregation + W40K-0194 antagonist + W40K-0196 trial-audience + W40K-0197 omnibus aggregation), Imperial Fists ×4 (W40K-0194 + W40K-0196 + W40K-0197 omnibus + W40K-0198 reference), Inquisition ×3 (W40K-0192 omnibus inheriting W40K-0190 Thaddeus + W40K-0196 trial-audience + W40K-0197 omnibus), Sisters of Battle ×2 (W40K-0196 + W40K-0197 omnibus), Howling Griffons ×2 (W40K-0193 + W40K-0197 omnibus), Crimson Fists ×2 (W40K-0191 + W40K-0192 omnibus), Dark Eldar ×2 (W40K-0191 + W40K-0192 omnibus), Necrons ×3 (W40K-0194 + W40K-0197 omnibus + W40K-0198 implicit-ancient-enemy → not tagged on W40K-0198 since the Lexicanum doesn't name them as Necrons explicitly), Orks ×2 (W40K-0193 + W40K-0197 omnibus), Imperial Guard ×2 (W40K-0191 + W40K-0192 omnibus), Adeptus Arbites ×2 (W40K-0199 + W40K-0200 — **first authority-layer Arbites-as-faction-class**), Rogue Traders ×1 (W40K-0200), Ecclesiarchy ×2 (W40K-0199 festival-context + W40K-0200 ambitious-clerics secondary-threat). *Locations*: Imperium ×10, the Phalanx ×3 (W40K-0196 + W40K-0197 omnibus + W40K-0198 Primaris-context implicit — only tagged on W40K-0196 + W40K-0197 to keep surface forms tight), Selaaca ×2 (W40K-0196 + W40K-0197 omnibus), Vanqualis ×2 (W40K-0193 + W40K-0197 omnibus), Hydraphur ×2 (W40K-0199 + W40K-0200), Kepris ×1 (W40K-0198). *Characters*: Sarpedon ×7 (W40K-0191 + W40K-0192 omnibus + W40K-0193 + W40K-0194 + W40K-0195 implicit-not-on-page + W40K-0196 + W40K-0197 omnibus — listed on six entries explicitly), Daenyathos ×3 (W40K-0195 POV + W40K-0196 antagonist-revealed + W40K-0197 omnibus), Iktinos ×2 (W40K-0196 antagonist + W40K-0197 omnibus), Tellos ×2 (W40K-0191 antagonist + W40K-0192 omnibus), Shira Calpurnia ×2 (W40K-0199 POV + W40K-0200 POV — **first female-POV authority-layer character in the Calpurnia line**), Lord Medell ×1 (W40K-0199), Hoyyon Phrax ×1 (W40K-0200 deceased), Yeceqath the Voice of All ×1 (W40K-0198). **Brief-071-Vertrag aktiv**: Override-Datei + Status-Log-Append in einem Commit ohne Co-Author-Trailer, keine sonstigen Datei-Edits. **Resolver-Pre-Note**: `cumulativeAfter=200`; **die nächste Iteration (`ssot-w40k-021`, W40K-0201..0210) trifft den natürlichen 50er-Stop** — `cumulativeBefore=200`, `200 % 50 == 0`, Resolver-Pause loud-stop guaranteed unless `skip-50-stop` marker. This batch is the last cleanly-produced batch before the resolver pause. Cowork-Empfehlung verstärkt seit ssot-w40k-017/018/019: surface-form-pile since the last Resolver-Pass in 069 now spans ssot-w40k-006..020 = 150 Bücher and includes the entire Necromunda Reynolds-imprint cluster + the modern Necromunda imprint cluster + the Last Chancers / Gothic War / Soul Drinkers opener cluster from ssot-w40k-019 + the full Soul Drinkers continuation cluster + the new Adeptus Arbites / Hydraphur surface-form-set from W40K-0199..0200. **Cross-batch alias-consolidation cases waiting for Resolver**: (1) Mad Donna / D'onne Ulanti (ssot-w40k-017 W40K-0162 + ssot-w40k-018 W40K-0177); (2) Kal Jerico (ssot-w40k-017 W40K-0164..0170 + ssot-w40k-018 W40K-0172 — 2005-2008 classic vs modern-imprint soft-reboot continuity question); (3) Lord Helmawr / Lord Gerontius Helmawr (ssot-w40k-017 assassinated + ssot-w40k-018 alive in modern continuity — distinct entities or soft-reboot revival); (4) Lieutenant Kage / the Burned Man (ssot-w40k-019 W40K-0185 dual-listing as deliberate narrative device); (5) Soul Drinkers Firstborn vs Soul Drinkers Primaris (ssot-w40k-019 + this batch's W40K-0189..0197 vs this batch's W40K-0198 — same chapter-name, different gene-seed-tier-cohort, fifteen-year publication-gap, post-Cicatrix-Maledictum continuity reboot). Five resolver-class problems waiting to be crystallised; the cumulative=200 natural pause is the right moment to write the Resolver-Brief.

overrides.synopsis: Years after his apparent death on Stratix Lumine, Tellos — once a Soul Drinker, now wholly given to Chaos — has returned at the head of his frenzied warriors, on an Imperial world already at war with Dark Eldar raiders. He carries the secrets of his old chapter with him, and Sarpedon must take what remains of his depleted Astartes to hunt his former brother one last time. A character-study of command under unbearable cost, and the close of the first Soul Drinkers trilogy.

The Soul Drinkers' rebellion against their own Chapter Master over a stolen relic ends in Excommunicate Traitoris status and the slow chaotic mutation of every warrior in the chapter. Librarian Sarpedon — soon to grow arachnid legs as the gene-seed twists — leads them through a fugitive war against the Inquisition's Thaddeus and the daemon-prince Teturact, and at last to a reckoning with Tellos, the former brother now wholly Chaos. The complete first trilogy in a single volume: a chapter-tragedy of pride, mutation, and the cost of choosing rebellion.

The Excommunicate Soul Drinkers answer an Ork invasion of Vanqualis, hoping to prove their loyalty to the Imperium that cast them out. Inside the chapter, a faction is ready to abandon the renegade-loyalist creed and openly embrace Chaos; outside, the Howling Griffons arrive as Imperial reinforcements who see them not as allies but as a renegade target. Sarpedon fights on three fronts at once: the Orks, his own splinter-traitors, and his fellow Astartes.

Bloodied from chapter-civil-war and pursued by the Imperium, the Soul Drinkers flee to the galactic rim, looking for refuge in dead worlds. Refuge becomes trap: the Necrons stir from their tombs to defend the silence, and an Adeptus Mechanicus fleet closes from behind, smashing the Brokenback — the chapter's captured Chaos-cruiser fortress — in the crossfire. A tight war-story shot through with cosmic-horror.

A prequel-novella told across centuries — Chaplain Daenyathos as a sergeant at the Second Siege of Terra, then his slow rise as the Soul Drinkers' philosopher-soldier and author of their Catechisms Martial, then his interment in a dreadnought and disappearance. Daenyathos has watched the Imperium long enough to despise it, and the Catechisms are not the guidance his chapter believed — they are the long-game seed of its undoing.

The Soul Drinkers have been taken alive by the Imperial Fists and brought aboard the Phalanx — the planetoid-fortress of Dorn's gene-sons — to stand trial for their crimes against the Imperium. Sarpedon defends his chapter before an audience of Astartes, Inquisitors, and Mechanicus, until a confession leads to a vault on Selaaca and Daenyathos is brought back to face trial beside the warriors he engineered. The series finale closes the chapter-tragedy at bittersweet cost.

The second half of the Soul Drinkers' arc, collected: the Ork-invasion of Vanqualis and the chapter-civil-war that splits the brothers between renegade-loyalist and openly Chaotic; the flight to the galactic rim where the Necrons stir and the Mechanicus pursues; and at last the Imperial Fists' capture of the chapter and the trial aboard the Phalanx, where Daenyathos's centuries-long corruption-scheme comes home and Sarpedon's tale ends. Three novels and short fiction in one volume — the slow disintegration of a chapter that chose rebellion.

An Indomitus-Crusade reconquest sends a chapter of Primaris Astartes — newborn legions bearing the name and heraldry of the Soul Drinkers lost before the Cicatrix Maledictum — to bring Kepris back to the Emperor's rule. The world has fallen to a cult led by the arch-heretic Yeceqath, the Voice of All, and beneath that conspiracy something far older waits. A tight standalone novella that re-establishes the chapter for the post-Maledictum era.

Newly promoted to arbiter senioris, Shira Calpurnia of the Adeptus Arbites arrives at Hydraphur and lands in a tangle of corruption, civil unrest, and Ecclesiarchy politics — opened by a string of assassination attempts on her own life. The trail leads to a noble house and a conspiracy hidden beneath a religious festival, climaxing in a bell-tower as Calpurnia keeps her quarry talking long enough to read him the law. A rare Arbites-led procedural-mystery in a political-thriller key.

The death of a rich and ancient Rogue Trader brings his Charter of Trade — a relic literally signed by the Emperor — to Hydraphur, where Calpurnia must see Hoyyon Phrax's will carried out under Imperial law. Rival heirs decide that due process be damned, and the administrators of a will must take up arms on the side of legal process — while ambitious clerics of the Ecclesiarchy, drawn by the relic, open a second front. The procedural shades into court-intrigue.
